title: cordia neowediana
intro: Cordia neowediana, also known as the Wild Orange, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Cordia neowediana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in digestion, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
